text:
Once upon a time in a sleepy town nestled between rolling hills and a meandering river, there thrived a sanctuary known as Whispering Woods. It wasn’t just any woods; it was the place where the whispers seemed louder than speech.

Our favorite story begins with Luna, a wise old owl who had seen many winters shift the leaves. Luna knew that every year was different, but one thing remained constant—the annual berry harvest. It was her job. Every autumn leaf was crunched in her beak, and out popped countless maroonberries.

One year, however, a shadow came over the woods. A dark cloud brooded overhead, and a thief was rumored. Luna had never dared speak of these rumors, choosing instead to believe in silent vigilance. Birds flew quicker, and even her usual dinner of squirrels was scarce.

On the night before the big harvest, something remarkable happened. Stars twinkled with a soft brilliance, and soft music danced through the branches—a melody so sweet it
